The Farmer
  • API Reference
  • farmer.mosaic — Mosaic
  • View page source

farmer.mosaic — Mosaic

A Mosaic represents a single band’s full-field survey image. It handles path validation, WCS reading, pixel loading, background estimation, and brick creation. You typically do not instantiate it directly — the top-level farmer functions do this internally.

class farmer.mosaic.Mosaic(band, load=False)[source]

Bases: BaseImage

A full-field survey image for a single photometric or detection band.

__init__(band, load=False) → None[source]

Initialise a Mosaic for the specified band.

Validates the band against conf.BANDS (or conf.DETECTION for the detection image), verifies that the science FITS file exists and has a readable WCS, and optionally loads the full image data into memory.

Parameters:

  • band – Band name (key in conf.BANDS) or 'detection'. Band names must not contain a '.' character.

  • load – If True, read all configured image arrays (science, weight, mask) and PSF model into memory. If False, only validate paths and load the WCS. Defaults to False.

Raises:

  • ValueError – If band contains a '.' character.

  • RuntimeError – If the science FITS header does not contain a valid WCS.

get_bands()[source]

Return the band(s) associated with this mosaic.

Returns:

Single-element array containing self.band.

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

get_figprefix(imgtype, band=None)[source]

Generate a filename prefix for output figures of this mosaic.

Parameters:

  • imgtype – Image type string (e.g. 'science', 'model').

  • band – Ignored for mosaics; self.band is always used.

Returns:

Prefix in the format '{band}_{imgtype}'.

Return type:

str

add_to_brick(brick)[source]

Cut out this mosaic’s data at the brick’s footprint and attach it.

Delegates to add_band(), which extracts Cutout2D sub-images centred on the brick position (including buffer) for every image type present in this mosaic.

Parameters:

brick – Brick object to receive the cut-out data.

Returns:

The same brick object, now containing this mosaic’s band.

Return type:

Brick

spawn_brick(brick_id=None, position=None, size=None, silent=False)[source]

Create a new blank brick and populate it from this mosaic.

Instantiates a fresh Brick (without loading from disk) and calls add_to_brick() to cut out the relevant sub-image. The brick position is derived either from brick_id (looked up on the detection WCS grid) or from explicit position/size arguments.

Parameters:

  • brick_id – Integer brick identifier (1-indexed). If provided, position and size must be None.

  • position – astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord of the brick centre. Used only when brick_id is None.

  • size – (dec_height, ra_width) tuple of angular astropy.units.Quantity values. Used only when brick_id is None.

  • silent – If True, suppress informational log messages. Defaults to False.

Returns:

Newly created brick populated with this mosaic’s band.

Return type:

Brick

extract(background=None)[source]

Detect sources across the full mosaic and build a catalog.

Calls the base-class _extract method on the full mosaic array, stores the resulting catalog in self.catalogs['science'] and the segmentation map in self.data['segmap'], then appends sequential id, ra, and dec columns.

Parameters:

background – Pre-computed background array to subtract before detection. When None no subtraction is performed. Defaults to None.

Returns:

None. Populates self.catalogs['science'], self.data['segmap'], and self.n_sources['science'].

identify_groups(radius=conf.DILATION_RADIUS, overwrite=False)[source]

Group nearby sources by morphologically dilating the segmentation map.

Converts radius from arcsec to pixels using the mosaic WCS, calls dilate_and_group(), and stores group_id and group_pop columns in self.catalogs['science'] and the group map in self.data['groupmap'].

Parameters:

  • radius – Dilation radius as an astropy.units.Quantity angle. Defaults to conf.DILATION_RADIUS.

  • overwrite – If True, replace existing group_id/group_pop columns; if False, add them as new columns. Defaults to False.
